skill.name: monday-automation
skill.description: Automate Monday.com boards, items, groups, columns, subitems, and updates through Rube MCP and Composio Monday tools.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents through safe Monday.com workflow automation using Rube MCP tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are false positives from Markdown code spans around Monday and Rube tool names. The skill is documentation-only, but it depends on an external Rube MCP endpoint and guides authenticated Monday.com write operations, including raw GraphQL mutations.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Monday.com Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create boards, create items, update columns, move items, and add updates through an authenticated Monday.com connection. This is legitimate automation, but mistakes can change live workspace data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly require an active Monday connection and list write-capable Monday tools. The risk is operational data modification, not arbitrary code execution.

medium_findings.title: Raw GraphQL Mutation Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends MONDAY_CREATE_OBJECT for raw GraphQL mutations, including operations without dedicated tools. The documentation cites create_subitem, delete_item, and archive_board as examples, so user confirmation is important before destructive changes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file names a raw GraphQL interface and includes examples that can create or remove Monday.com objects. Legitimate use is possible, but the blast radius is broader than typed tool calls.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External MCP Endpoint
low_findings.description: The setup instructions tell users to add the Rube MCP server URL. This is an expected third-party service dependency, but users should verify the endpoint and understand that Monday data flows through that integration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 20 contains a direct HTTPS endpoint for Rube MCP. The URL is intentional setup guidance rather than covert exfiltration.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backtick-delimited tool names as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The file is Markdown documentation and contains no script body or executable shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown inline code spans and tables that describe MCP tool names and parameters. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command interpreter context is present.

low_findings.title: Blocker Pattern Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance findings do not match the surrounding text. The cited lines describe the skill description, Monday group listing, column type guidance, and template access checks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain ordinary Monday.com workflow guidance and no cryptographic function, host inspection command, port scan, or network probing instruction.

dangerous_patterns.title: Raw GraphQL Write Interface
dangerous_patterns.description: MONDAY_CREATE_OBJECT accepts full GraphQL mutation strings. It should be used only after resolving IDs and confirming the intended workspace change with the user.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly documented as a raw GraphQL endpoint for actions without dedicated tools. This increases the chance of broad or destructive changes if prompts are ambiguous.

content.user_title: Automate Monday.com Workflows with Rube MCP
content.value_statement: Monday.com automation often fails when agents use stale schemas or wrong IDs. This skill provides ordered Rube MCP workflows for boards, items, columns, groups, subitems, and updates.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to connect Rube MCP and verify an active Monday.com OAuth connection.
Provides tool sequences for creating boards, columns, groups, items, subitems, and updates.
Explains how to resolve board, group, column, workspace, and item names to IDs.
Documents column value formats for status, date, people, text, numbers, and timeline fields.
Covers pagination behavior for Monday.com boards, workspaces, and item pages.
Identifies when to use typed Monday tools versus raw GraphQL mutations.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Monday.com connection before any workflow can run.
Does not include executable code or a local Monday.com API client.
Raw GraphQL examples require careful validation before use on live boards.
Current tool schemas must be fetched before each workflow because MCP tools can change.

use_cases.title: Set Up Project Boards
use_cases.description: Create a new Monday.com board, add groups, define columns, and confirm the workspace structure before work starts.

use_cases.title: Maintain Task Data
use_cases.description: Find existing items, update statuses, move tasks between groups, and add comments without manually navigating Monday.com.

use_cases.title: Build Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Use repeatable tool sequences for ID resolution, pagination, column formatting, subitems, and GraphQL fallback actions.

prompt_templates.title: List My Monday Workspaces
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the monday-automation skill to verify Rube MCP, search for current Monday tools, and list my available Monday.com workspaces.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Project Board
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the monday-automation skill to create a private Monday.com board named Product Launch, then add status, date, people, and text columns.

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the monday-automation skill to find the Marketing Tasks board, locate overdue items, update their status, and add a short update comment.

prompt_templates.title: Create Subitems with GraphQL
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the monday-automation skill to resolve the parent item ID, confirm the target task, and prepare a Monday GraphQL subitem mutation for review before execution.

output_examples.input: Create a delivery board with groups for backlog, active work, and completed work.
output_examples.output: The agent verifies the Monday connection, resolves the workspace, checks for duplicate boards, creates the board, and adds the requested groups.

output_examples.input: Move the website redesign task to In Progress and set its due date.
output_examples.output: The agent resolves the board, item, group, and column IDs, then updates the status and date using the correct Monday column formats.

output_examples.input: Show me all subitems for three launch tasks.
output_examples.output: The agent finds the parent item IDs, calls the subitem retrieval tool, and summarizes the returned subitems with their visible column values.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so every call uses the current Monday.com schema.
Resolve names to exact IDs before creating, moving, or updating Monday.com records.
Ask for confirmation before raw GraphQL mutations or actions that delete, archive, or bulk update data.
anti_patterns: Do not guess board, group, column, workspace, or item IDs from display names.
Do not use raw GraphQL when a typed Monday MCP tool can perform the action.
Do not run updates against production boards before confirming the target workspace and item list.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to Monday.com?
faq.answer: No. It guides an AI agent to use Rube MCP and the Composio Monday toolkit.

faq.question: Do I need a Monday.com account?
faq.answer: Yes. You need an active Monday.com connection through Rube MCP before workflows can modify data.

faq.question: Can it create boards and items?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents tool sequences for creating boards, groups, columns, items, subitems, and updates.

faq.question: Why must the agent search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first reduces parameter errors and stale automation steps.

faq.question: Is raw GraphQL required?
faq.answer: Only for actions without dedicated tools, such as some subitem or archive workflows. Confirm these actions first.

faq.question: Is this safe for production boards?
faq.answer: Use it with confirmation steps. It can guide real authenticated changes to live Monday.com data.
